reviews.review_msg: She is so kind and took her in the spring, everything on the exams and assignments were all she taught in the class. Don't worries if you are afraid of Q courses, she is really a great choice!
She is excellent and want students success. If you pay the efforts to do more practice, you will get a very good grade.
Been 10 days dince the final exam has been written, still have not recieved a grade, and for all the other classes for which the final was done the grade has been released.

The professor was caring; however, the course structure was unexpected, the professor didn't show up for the midterm nor the final. The midterm length was told to be 1.5 hours. However upon, arrival was 2.5 hours. The course concepts were presented a bit confusingly, and the final and midterm were lengthy but were fair overall.

I'm gonna be honest I only went to 3 lectures the entire semester so I can't comment much on how her teaching is. As long as you read the textbook and do all the practice questions as well as the practice exams, you'll do great. Exams are the exact same as the practice exams except different wording/numbers.

Prof didn't show up for midterm AND final; Very simple concepts were presented in a very complex way (both in lectures and lecture notes); Excruciatingly long 16-20 page midterm and final; Couldn't answer students' questions during lectures; Professor seems caring but is not fit to teach. She seems confused about the course content herself.

she's not a bad prof tbh, the three hour lectures are a bit long sometimes and can be hard to follow. But she tries her best to explain material! you can tell she cares, exams are fair and based on lectures and assignments

Quite a dry lecture and class. We spend 30mins or more going over the same simple questions and it seems like she does not know the answers to questions students ask. Although I think she cares for the class and is a nice person, I cannot recommend this class.
Everything is super unorganized! Final Exam is cumulative, not like the other (They even provided the Formula Sheet).
It was extremely chaotic. She herself didn't even know what she wanted to teach.
OMG….to much to digest at once. It's like watching a movie. Non stop. Probably worse prof until now.

Possibly the worst professor I've ever had. Unorganized lectures. Spends the first 15 min of every class figuring out what she even wants to say. Glosses over important concepts and explains in such a way that makes the simplest concept the hardest thing you'v ever learned. I suggest you save your time and money and not take the course with her.
Goes too fast in class. Classes are not organized. would rather learn by myself at home. make simple concepts too complicated. Not at all recommended.
She is a nice prof, know how to teach well. Could say a good choice

caring and very kind
Lectures are somewhat boring and sleepy but if you pay attention, it's understandable
Exams are the same as practice questions, so practice as much as you can, and mcqs just remember it
The rest: depends on your luck and the curve of the class
She is a great processor and understands the material she's teaching. Exams are fair.

She's very through and her course material preps you well for exams. Homework can be a little tough since they wont tell you what you got right or wrong until everything is submitted. Most exam questions are similar to practice problems so if you put in the effort, you'll do well.
Nice prof and TA, you will be find if you attend the lecture every week and go to the open lab weekly.

Her lectures are very thorough and she makes sure that students understand the topic. She even omitted some sections of the course because she would spend extra time explaining topics that she believed students needed help on. Her exams are very fair, and I never had to look at the textbook, the lecture slides were enough.

Don't take Bus 336 with her. She does not know how to deliver course material to the class and you will end up self-teaching. She doesn't go over any questions during class that could help with the assignment. Going to open lab saved me. If I solely relied on her lectures and teaching I would have done even worse on the assignments and exams.
